title: Pogonolepis muelleriana
intro: Pogonolepis muelleriana, commonly known as the bushy pug, is a plant species in the family Compositae, native to Australia. The plant has been traditionally used in Australian folk medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and gastrointestinal issues. The bioactive constituents of Pogonolepis muelleriana include sesquiterpene lactones, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with this plant are teas and infusions, although it may also be used in the form of tinctures or capsules to leverage its health benefits.
taxonomy: Pogonolepis muelleriana is a species of plant in the family Asteraceae. It is native to Western Australia. The classification of Pogonolepis muelleriana is as follows: kingdom Plantae, clade Angiosperms, clade Eudicots, clade Asterids, order Asterales, family Asteraceae, genus Pogonolepis, species P. muelleriana. This classification reflects the plant's evolutionary relationships and membership within the broader plant kingdom. Pogonolepis muelleriana's classification is an essential part of understanding its unique characteristics and position within the plant kingdom.
